commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the protected maintainer draft must resolve the shell-boundary acceptance and canonical-PR choice; there is no narrow automated repair to request.; Needs attention: The code does not expose a token interpolation bug, but the added WSL2 shell wrapper is a real security-boundary decision for maintainers. (sha=a56a6487331d4c74cfc3ab6445f1960a55912db2)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a WSL2-only static `/bin/sh -c` clipboard bridge and tests that tokenized dashboard URLs stay on stdin while non-WSL fallback order remains unchanged.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +41. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main’s clipboard implementation directly attempts `clip.exe`, and the linked WSL2 report plus the PR’s WSL2 Crabbox readback proof give a high-confidence reproduction path, although I did not rerun live WSL2 proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the protected maintainer draft must resolve the shell-boundary acceptance and canonical-PR choice; there is no narrow automated repair to request.

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary

- route WSL2 clipboard copies through a static `/bin/sh -c` bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- keep dashboard/token URLs on stdin instead of shell argv, and avoid Bash login/profile startup
- preserve the existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, PowerShell, and non-WSL2 fallback order
- tighten the regression test so token substrings cannot hide inside argv elements

Fixes #88080.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.test.ts` - passed after rebase to current `origin/main`, 34 focused tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `crabbox run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode wsl2 --no-hydrate --no-sync --label wsl-clipboard-88365-readback-fresh --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --script-stdin` - passed on AWS Windows/WSL2 Crabbox, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_065a8d1e6d5a`, run `run_d8528bc8e381`; generated probe wrote through `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and matched `Get-Clipboard`
- `crabbox run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode normal --no-hydrate --no-sync --label native-windows-clipboard-88365-readback-fresh --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --script-stdin` - passed on AWS native Windows Crabbox, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_0eccf7451a24`, run `run_e94d2893eb27`; generated probe wrote through direct `%SystemRoot%\System32\clip.exe` and matched `Get-Clipboard`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --help` - blocked locally before remote execution because the wrapper attempted to prepare a full local sync checkout and the machine had insufficient disk; the failed temp worktree was removed. The previous AWS Linux `pnpm check:changed` attempt for this PR was blocked by an unrelated current-main Discord boundary dts error after core/core-test typecheck and earlier guards passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WSL2 dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct non-shell `clip.exe` argv execution or a Bash login shell. The WSL2 path uses `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and passes the copied dashboard URL through stdin.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Windows/WSL2 host for shell-bridge clipboard write/readback; AWS Crabbox native Windows host for direct Windows clipboard write/readback; focused local Vitest/format/lint/diff-check; branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, oxfmt, touched-file oxlint, diff-check, branch autoreview, AWS Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback, and AWS native Windows clipboard readback commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: the Windows/WSL2 run wrote a generated probe string through `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and read it back with Windows PowerShell `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched exactly. The native Windows run wrote a generated probe through direct `%SystemRoot%\System32\clip.exe` and read it back with `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched. The WSL2 regression test asserts copied values stay on stdin rather than argv, and the non-WSL fallback-order test keeps the existing `pbcopy`, `xclip`, `wl-copy`, `clip.exe`, and PowerShell order.

Observed result after fix: Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback passed on rebased commit `a56a6487331d4c74cfc3ab6445f1960a55912db2`; native Windows clipboard readback passed; focused tests, touched-file format/lint, diff-check, and autoreview passed.

What was not tested: `openclaw dashboard --no-open` end-to-end with a real token URL on a user workstation. The runner proof directly exercised the WSL2 clipboard bridge that dashboard calls, using non-secret generated probe values. A fresh broad `pnpm check:changed` was not completed in this local lane because the repo Crabbox wrapper attempted to materialize a full local sync checkout and hit local disk limits before remote execution.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
